name: Pexels API
what_it_does: Free API for searching and downloading high-quality, royalty-free stock photos and videos from Pexels' curated library of millions of assets.
use_cases: Automatically sourcing images for blog posts or content generation agents
Finding relevant photos for presentation or document generation
Building image search features without licensing complexity
Downloading stock video B-roll for video production workflows
Generating illustrated reports or visual content with contextual imagery
not_for: Editorial photography (Pexels is generic stock, not news photography)
Very specific niche imagery that stock photos don't cover
High-volume downloads without attribution (check license per use)
best_when: An agent needs to automatically source free, high-quality images or videos for content without managing licensing.
avoid_when: You need editorial, niche, or brand-specific imagery, or require guaranteed content for specific subjects.

auth.notes: API key in Authorization header. Free plan requires application approval. Single key per application.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 200 requests/hour; monthly limits not published but fair use applies
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise plan available for high-volume commercial use
pricing.notes: Completely free for most uses. Must provide attribution to Pexels/photographer unless on enterprise plan. Application approval required (usually auto-approved).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP status codes with JSON error body. 429 rate limit errors include no Retry-After header.
known_agent_gotchas: Attribution to Pexels and photographer is legally required for free tier use in most contexts
Search results are curated and may not return results for very specific or niche queries
Photo download URLs are direct CDN links that expire - agents should download immediately, not cache URLs long-term
200 requests/hour limit can be hit quickly if searching many topics in a content generation pipeline
Videos require separate endpoint from photos despite similar search syntax

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key. No scope granularity. Free for commercial use with attribution. Rate limits enforced per key. Images are royalty-free but proper attribution may be required.
